question: Under the pinned Braess diamond (nodes s,a,b,t; base edges e1=s->a, e2=a->t, e3=s->b, e4=b->t; optional bridge e5=a->b enabling route s->a->b->t; unit demand D=1 routed to the UNIQUE Wardrop user-equilibrium under affine non-decreasing latencies l_e(x)=a_e*x+b_e with a_e>=0, equilibrium and total cost C=sum_e x_e*(a_e*x_e+b_e) computed exactly), how OFTEN and by HOW MUCH does adding the bridge edge RAISE the selfish-equilibrium total travel cost across the pre-registered affine-latency census — measured as f_A, the paradox frequency (cost_with > cost_without) over the exhaustive integer census (a_e,b_e) in {0,1,2}^2 on the four base edges and (a5,b5) in {0,1}^2 on the bridge, 9^4*4 = 26,244 raw fixtures with 0-cost-without fixtures excluded and the effective N reported (exact fractions.Fraction, no PRNG), together with median-r and max-r among the paradox fixtures (r = cost_with/cost_without), AND f_S, the pooled paradox frequency over the seeded continuous-coefficient robustness arm (a_e,b_e ~ U[0,2], random.Random seeds 20260740/20260741/20260742/20260743, documented float tolerance on the comparison) — and does that clear the APPROVE band? Bands, registered BEFORE any code: APPROVE ('an added-edge Braess audit is warranted') iff f_A >= 0.15 OR median-r-among-paradox >= 1.15; REJECT ('added edges safe-by-default in this class') iff f_A <= 0.03 AND max-r <= 1.05; NULL (the straddle) otherwise — and NULL-by-arm-disagreement if the arm-agreement gate (|f_S - f_A| <= 1.0 pp AND both arms yield the SAME APPROVE/REJECT/NULL call) is not met.

source.proposal_pin: idea-engine main b11e258 (landed via idea-engine PR #290)
source.idea_entry: ideas/fleet/braess-paradox-added-edge-2026-07-13.md @ idea-engine main b11e258 (proposal's own blob link @ f1733830a5bd9070e84374e4a6c3accbbf7652a1)
source.rotation_slot: ORDER 004 rule-3 COMPLETELY-UNRELATED-domain rotation slot, round 2 — transportation / congestion routing games (Wardrop selfish-user equilibrium), a fresh fleet-external domain DIFFERENT from round 1's social choice (PROPOSAL 017 -> VERDICT 019); fully hermetic per the PROPOSAL 017-023 precedent: both arms construct their entire world in-sim, zero repo/network reads in the verdict session, every fixture below is a pinned constant committed with the sim

topology.latency: l_e(x) = a_e*x + b_e, a_e >= 0 (non-decreasing affine); route flows (f1, f2, f3) >= 0 with f1+f2+f3 = 1; edge flows x1 = f1+f3, x2 = f1, x3 = f2, x4 = f2+f3, x5 = f3
topology.equilibrium: Wardrop user equilibrium = minimizer of the convex Beckmann potential Phi = sum_e (a_e*x_e^2/2 + b_e*x_e) over feasible unit s->t flows; every used route's latency equals the minimum route latency (verified exactly per fixture); for monotone affine latencies ALL equilibria share every edge latency and the total cost (essential uniqueness), so any verified equilibrium yields THE equilibrium cost
topology.total_cost: C = sum_e x_e*(a_e*x_e + b_e) = the common equilibrium route latency times demand

arm_A.name: exhaustive integer census (exact, no PRNG)
arm_A.coefficient_grid_base_edges: (a_e, b_e) in {0,1,2} x {0,1,2} independently for e1..e4
arm_A.census_order: itertools.product over (e1, e2, e3, e4, e5) coefficient pairs, each pair in lexicographic (a, b) order — pinned enumeration order
arm_A.degenerate_skip: fixtures with cost_without == 0 are excluded from numerator and denominator; the skip count and effective N are reported
arm_A.arithmetic: fractions.Fraction end to end — f_A, median-r, max-r are platform-independent exact rationals
arm_A.statistics: f_A = (# fixtures with cost_with > cost_without) / N; among paradox fixtures r = cost_with / cost_without; median-r (even count: arithmetic mean of the two middle values, exact) and max-r

arm_S.name: seeded continuous-coefficient robustness (floats, pinned seeds)
arm_S.distribution: a_e = 2*rng.random(), b_e = 2*rng.random() for all five edges — U[0,2] each, 10 uniforms per draw in edge order e1(a,b), e2(a,b), e3(a,b), e4(a,b), e5(a,b)
arm_S.float_tolerance: a draw counts as a paradox iff cost_with > cost_without*(1 + 1e-9) + 1e-12 (relative + absolute guard, so knife-edge float noise cannot inflate f_S); Wardrop-condition verification tolerance 1e-9; degenerate skip cost_without <= 1e-12 (measure zero under continuous draws; counted and reported)
arm_S.statistic: f_S = pooled paradox frequency across the four seeds (per-seed counts also reported)

decision_rule.APPROVE: f_A >= 0.15 OR median-r-among-paradox >= 1.15  ('an added-edge Braess audit is warranted')
decision_rule.REJECT: f_A <= 0.03 AND max-r <= 1.05  ('added edges safe-by-default in this class')
decision_rule.NULL: anything not fully meeting APPROVE or REJECT (the straddle), OR NULL-by-arm-disagreement
decision_rule.band_exclusivity: APPROVE and REJECT are mutually exclusive: REJECT's f_A <= 0.03 < 0.15 blocks APPROVE's first leg and REJECT's max-r <= 1.05 forces median-r <= 1.05 < 1.15, blocking the second — so no evaluation-order ambiguity exists
decision_rule.arm_agreement_gate: non-NULL ruling requires |f_S - f_A| <= 1.0 pp AND both arms yield the SAME APPROVE/REJECT/NULL call; otherwise NULL-by-arm-disagreement (a legitimate finalized outcome, not a retry)
decision_rule.band_comparisons: exact in Arm A (Fraction vs Fraction: f_A vs 3/100 and 15/100, r vs 23/20 and 21/20); Arm S comparisons in float against the same constants

consequences_pre_registered.APPROVE: when adding a shortcut/extra link to any selfishly-routed system, run a Braess check before shipping it, citing this class's measured rate — added capacity is NOT monotonically good
consequences_pre_registered.REJECT: treat added edges as safe-by-default in this class and skip the design-time Braess audit
consequences_pre_registered.NULL: the honest outcome — the risk is real but not common/large enough for a blanket rule; audit only high-stakes networks case-by-case, and neither 'real design hazard' nor 'textbook curiosity' may be cited as settled

model_basis.behavioral_assumption: selfish Wardrop user equilibrium (each user minimizes own latency) over affine latencies with coefficients from a uniform grid/range ({0,1,2} integers in Arm A, U[0,2] in Arm S); the measured frequency is a property of THIS routing behavior on THIS coefficient distribution
model_basis.most_likely_to_flip: system-optimal routing (minimize TOTAL latency): under it Braess's paradox cannot occur at all (adding an edge weakly lowers the system optimum), so f -> 0; a softer flip is any non-uniform coefficient prior concentrated away from the paradox-prone corners

analytic_gates_run_invalid_on_failure.g1_wardrop_vi: at every computed equilibrium (both networks, every Arm A fixture exactly; every Arm S draw within tolerance): flows feasible and every route with positive flow has latency equal to the minimum route latency
analytic_gates_run_invalid_on_failure.g2_affine_braess_bound: every Arm A paradox ratio satisfies r <= 4/3 EXACTLY — the known worst-case Braess/affine bound; any violation is a solver bug by theorem
analytic_gates_run_invalid_on_failure.g3_reversal_symmetry: the reversal involution sigma(e1,e2,e3,e4,e5) = (e4,e3,e2,e1,e5) (reverse all edges, swap s and t, relabel a'=b) preserves cost_without and cost_with on every census fixture — asserted pairwise over the full census
analytic_gates_run_invalid_on_failure.g4_unused_bridge_invariance: cost_with != cost_without implies x5 > 0 at the with-bridge equilibrium; and if the without-bridge equilibrium already satisfies the with-bridge Wardrop conditions (R3 latency >= common latency), cost_with == cost_without — both exact
analytic_gates_run_invalid_on_failure.g5_reproducibility: CPython minor pinned to 3.11 and asserted; stdout + results.json byte-identical across two complete process runs by external diff
analytic_gates_run_invalid_on_failure.g6_draw_count_sentinel: Arm S consumes exactly 10 uniforms per draw; after each seed's leg, a fresh random.Random(seed) advanced 10*n_draws times must produce the same next random() as the leg's stream

twin_evaluator.arm_A: an independently written support-enumeration solver (enumerate nonempty route-support patterns; solve the latency-equalization linear system in exact Fractions; screen by feasibility and the Wardrop conditions; degenerate/underdetermined systems are skipped because every extreme equilibrium then lies on a smaller support, and singleton supports are always solvable) runs on the FULL census, both networks; asserts (i) it finds at least one equilibrium per fixture-network and (ii) EVERY equilibrium it finds has cost exactly equal to the primary solver's (essential uniqueness makes this the correctness contract)
twin_evaluator.arm_S: the same twin in float arithmetic on every draw with index % 199 == 0 within each seed, agreement within 1e-6 on both costs
twin_evaluator.primary_solver: Beckmann candidate enumeration: the 3 simplex vertices, up to 3 clamped edge-minimizers (each edge's directional derivative is affine; a root is a candidate only when the quadratic coefficient is positive), and the interior stationary point when the exact 2x2 gradient system is nonsingular and lands inside the simplex; the Phi-argmin is then VERIFIED against the Wardrop conditions (gate g1) — the verification, not the construction, carries the proof

hand_derived_pins.name: pin_A_canonical_braess
hand_derived_pins.derivation_without: L1(f1) = l1(f1) + l2(f1) = f1 + 1; L2 = l3 + l4 = 1 + (1 - f1); equalize: f1 + 1 = 2 - f1 -> f1 = 1/2; common latency 3/2; cost_without = 3/2
hand_derived_pins.derivation_with: all flow on R3: x1 = x5 = x4 = 1; L3 = 1 + 0 + 1 = 2; check L1 = l1(1) + l2(0) = 1 + 1 = 2 >= 2 and L2 = 1 + 1 = 2 >= 2 — Wardrop holds; cost_with = 2
hand_derived_pins.note: this in-census fixture realizes the affine worst case r = 4/3 exactly, so REJECT's max-r <= 1.05 leg is decidable from the census alone

hand_derived_pins.name: pin_B_constant_no_paradox
hand_derived_pins.derivation: all edge latencies constant 1; without: L1 = L2 = 2 for every split, cost_without = 2; with: L3 = 1 + 0 + 1 = 2 as well, every route constant 2, cost_with = 2; equal costs -> NOT a paradox (strict > required)

hand_derived_pins.name: pin_C_bridge_helps
hand_derived_pins.derivation: without: L1 = 0 + 2 = 2, L2 = 2 + 0 = 2 for every split, cost_without = 2; with: R3 latency = 0 + 1 + 0 = 1 constant < 2, all flow on R3, L1 = 0 + 2 = 2 >= 1, L2 = 2 + 0 = 2 >= 1 — Wardrop holds; cost_with = 1

expected.note: bridge strictly HELPS here — the sim must measure improvement as readily as harm

intake_time_decisions_disclosed: 1. n_draws_per_seed = 25,000 (pooled 100,000): at plausible f ~ 0.03 the binomial SE on f_S is ~0.05 pp, two orders inside the 1.0 pp arm-agreement gate. DISCLOSURE: a throwaway scratchpad feasibility probe (not this runner, not committed) ran the census and the pinned seeds at this n BEFORE this file was committed, to time the exact-Fraction solve and validate the solver approach; every decision constant above (grids, seeds, bands, gate) was already pinned by the idea file, so no free parameter remained tunable by that peek, and n_draws cannot move f_S materially (it only shrinks an SE already 20x inside the gate)

2. float tolerance (Arm S): paradox iff cost_with > cost_without*(1 + 1e-9) + 1e-12; VI tolerance 1e-9; degenerate skip at cost_without <= 1e-12 — Arm A is float-free so the headline census numbers carry no tolerance at all

3. Arm S call convention: the proposal's arm-agreement gate requires each arm's APPROVE/REJECT/NULL call; Arm S's call applies the SAME registered band constants to its own pooled statistics (f_S, median-r_S, max-r_S)
4. median convention: even paradox count -> arithmetic mean of the two middle r values (exact Fraction in Arm A)

5. equilibrium method: Beckmann candidate enumeration + exact per-fixture Wardrop verification (see twin_evaluator.primary_solver); correctness rests on the verification plus essential uniqueness of equilibrium cost for monotone latencies, NOT on trusting the construction
6. twin stride (Arm S): every 199th draw per seed — the full-census exact twin already covers the decision-carrying arm end to end
7. reporting-only breakdown: f_A conditional on each bridge cell (a5,b5) is reported for reading, cannot flip any band (the registered statistics pool the census)
outputs_registered: N (effective census after the 0-cost-without skip) and the skip count
median-r and max-r among Arm A paradox fixtures, exact + float
f_S pooled + per-seed counts (and Arm S median-r_S/max-r_S for its call)
arm-agreement check: |f_S - f_A| <= 1.0 pp AND same call
the APPROVE/REJECT/NULL call per arm and the final ruling against the registered bands
model-basis caveat restated in the verdict
